chapter_title.en: HADEETH ABOUT THE PEOPLE ON THE DAY OF JUDGEMENT

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 28)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 28)
text: كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام) إِذَا أَخَذَ كِتَابَ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) فَنَظَرَ فِيهِ قَالَ مَنْ يُطِيقُ هَذَا مَنْ يُطِيقُ ذَا قَالَ ثُمَّ يَعْمَلُ بِهِ وَ كَانَ إِذَا قَامَ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ تَغَيَّرَ لَوْنُهُ حَتَّى يُعْرَفَ ذَلِكَ فِي وَجْهِهِ وَ مَا أَطَاقَ أَحَدٌ عَمَلَ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) مِنْ وُلْدِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ إِلَّا عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام).

en.hubeali: H 14620 – Ali, from his father and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al-Fazl Bin Shazaan altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abd Al-Rahmaan Bin Al-Hajjaaj, and Hafs Bin Al-Bakhtary, and Salmat Bayya Al-Sabiry, who has narrated the following:

Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘Whenever Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws used to take the Book of Aliasws, heasws would look into it and say: ‘Who can endure this, who can endure this?’ Then heasws said: ‘Who would act upon it, and when heasws would stand for the Prayer, hisasws colour would change to the extent that it would become apparent in hisasws face, and no one can endure the actions of Aliasws from hisasws sonsasws from after himasws except for Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws’.
en.sarwar: 172. Ali, from his father and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al-Fazl Bin Shazaan altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abd Al-Rahmaan Bin Al-Hajjaaj, and Hafs Bin Al-Bakhtary, and Salmat Bayya Al-Sabiry, who has narrated the following: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: ‘Whenever Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Husayn (a.s.) used to take the Book of Ali (a.s.), he (a.s.) would look into it and say: ‘Who can endure this, who can endure this?’ Then he (a.s.) said: ‘Who would act upon it, and when he (a.s.) would stand for the Prayer, his (a.s.) colour would change to the extent that it would become apparent in his (a.s.) face, and no one can endure the actions of Ali (a.s.) from his (a.s.) sons (a.s.) from after him (a.s.) except for Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Husayn (a.s.)’.
